file_name: Magenta Therapeutics, Inc. - Master Development and Manufacturing Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: 1.14 "Latent Defect" means a defect which could have been detected (but was not) by the analytical test methods in operation at the date of shipment to Magenta, attributable to an act or omission of Bachem that causes a Product to fail to conform to the Specifications, which may not be discoverable upon the inspection and testing which Magenta would have been expected to carry out in its ordinary course of business, but is discovered at a later time.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.14 "Latent Defect" means a defect which is discoverable by the analytical test methods in operation at the date of shipment to Magenta, attributable to an act or omission of Bachem that causes a Product to fail to conform to the Specifications, which may be discoverable upon the inspection and testing which Magenta would have been expected to carry out in its ordinary course of business, and is discovered at a later time.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines 'Latent Defect' as a defect that 'could have been detected (but was not)' and 'may not be discoverable', while the modified text describes it as 'discoverable by the analytical test methods' and 'may be discoverable'. This creates an in-text contradiction as Section 1.14 now states that the latent defect can be found when originally it meant it couldn't be.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.14 "Latent Defect" means a defect which is discoverable by the analytical test methods in operation at the date of shipment to Magenta, attributable to an act or omission of Bachem that causes a Product to fail to conform to the Specifications, which may be discoverable upon the inspection and testing which Magenta would have been expected to carry out in its ordinary course of business, and is discovered at a later time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 1.14 directly contradicts the original meaning of 'Latent Defect' by stating that it *is* discoverable by analytical test methods, whereas the original text defined it as something that 'could have been detected (but was not)' and 'may not be discoverable'. This creates a clear inconsistency as Section 1.14 now states that the latent defect can be found when originally it meant it couldn't be. This creates an in-text contradiction as the Contradicted text states 'Latent Defect' means 'discoverable' which contradicts its earlier meaning. This creates a structural flaw with contradictory implications, based on the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 5.13 Restrictions on Bachem. Bachem agrees to supply the Product(s) identified in each applicable Project Plan to Magenta pursuant to the terms and conditions of this Agreement and any applicable Project Plans. During the Initial Term and any Renewal Term, Bachem agrees not to sell, supply or otherwise distribute [***] for any clinical or commercial use to any Third Party without Magenta's prior written consent, for so long as Bachem remains Magenta's primary supplier of [***] for the Initial Term and any Renewal Term.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.13 Restrictions on Bachem. Bachem agrees to supply the Product(s) identified in each applicable Project Plan to Magenta pursuant to the terms and conditions of this Agreement and any applicable Project Plans. During the Initial Term and any Renewal Term, Bachem may sell, supply or otherwise distribute [***] for any clinical or commercial use to any Third Party without Magenta's prior written consent, for so long as Bachem remains Magenta's primary supplier of [***] for the Initial Term and any Renewal Term.
perturbation.explanation: The original text prevents Bachem from supplying product to third parties. The modified text now allows Bachem to supply product to third parties, creating an in-text contradiction in Section 5.13 with its prior sentence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.13 Restrictions on Bachem. Bachem agrees to supply the Product(s) identified in each applicable Project Plan to Magenta pursuant to the terms and conditions of this Agreement and any applicable Project Plans. During the Initial Term and any Renewal Term, Bachem may sell, supply or otherwise distribute [***] for any clinical or commercial use to any Third Party without Magenta's prior written consent, for so long as Bachem remains Magenta's primary supplier of [***] for the Initial Term and any Renewal Term.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 5.13 prohibits Bachem from selling or supplying the product to third parties. The changed text in Section 5.13 now permits Bachem to sell or supply the product to third parties. This creates a direct contradiction within Section 5.13, as the same section both allows and disallows the same action. This directly contradicts the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: 7.2 Termination. This Agreement or any Project Plan may be terminated: (a) by Magenta for any reason upon [***] written notice to Bachem; (b) by either Party if the other Party materially breaches a provision of this Agreement or a Project Plan, and fails to cure such breach within [***] following receipt of written notification of such breach from the non-breaching Party; (c) by either Party, immediately, if the other Party becomes insolvent, is dissolved or liquidated, makes a general assignment for the benefit of its creditors, or files or has filed against it, a petition in bankruptcy that is not dismissed within sixty days after filing, or has a receiver appointed for a substantial part of its assets; and (d) by a Party or the Parties pursuant to Section 13. In the event of termination pursuant to Section 7.2(a) or a termination by Bachem pursuant to Section 7.2(b), Bachem shall be compensated for Services rendered up to the date of termination. In the event of any other termination, the Parties shall negotiate in good faith to determine the appropriate amount to be paid by Magenta to Bachem (or refunded to Magenta by Bachem, as the case may be), in light of the circumstances of such termination, in compensation for all Services rendered in accordance with this Agreement. In the event of Bachem's inability to supply the Product or a material breach by Bachem pursuant to Section 7.2(b), Bachem shall provide, without additional charge to Magenta, sufficient information and technology pertaining to its Services to Magenta and/or its technically competent designee, such that Magenta and/or its technically competent designee are enabled to continue Development Work and manufacture of the Product. The termination of any Project Plan may be independent of the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.2 Termination. This Agreement or any Project Plan may be terminated: (a) by Magenta for any reason upon [***] written notice to Bachem; (b) by either Party if the other Party materially breaches a provision of this Agreement or a Project Plan, and fails to cure such breach within [***] following receipt of written notification of such breach from the non-breaching Party; (c) by either Party, immediately, if the other Party becomes insolvent, is dissolved or liquidated, makes a general assignment for the benefit of its creditors, or files or has filed against it, a petition in bankruptcy that is not dismissed within sixty days after filing, or has a receiver appointed for a substantial part of its assets; and (d) by a Party or the Parties pursuant to Section 13. In the event of termination pursuant to Section 7.2(a) or a termination by Bachem pursuant to Section 7.2(b), Bachem shall be compensated for Services rendered up to the date of termination. In the event of any other termination, the Parties shall negotiate in bad faith to determine the appropriate amount to be paid by Magenta to Bachem (or refunded to Magenta by Bachem, as the case may be), in light of the circumstances of such termination, in compensation for all Services rendered in accordance with this Agreement. In the event of Bachem's inability to supply the Product or a material breach by Bachem pursuant to Section 7.2(b), Bachem shall provide, without additional charge to Magenta, sufficient information and technology pertaining to its Services to Magenta and/or its technically competent designee, such that Magenta and/or its technically competent designee are enabled to continue Development Work and manufacture of the Product. The termination of any Project Plan may be independent of the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires that after a project is terminated, the parties must negotiate in 'good faith' to determine the amount to be paid. The modified text suggests that the parties must negotiate in 'bad faith' after the project is terminated, thus leading to an in-text contradiction and undermining the integrity of Section 7.2 as a whole, particularly since other subsections depend on it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.2 Termination. This Agreement or any Project Plan may be terminated: (a) by Magenta for any reason upon [***] written notice to Bachem; (b) by either Party if the other Party materially breaches a provision of this Agreement or a Project Plan, and fails to cure such breach within [***] following receipt of written notification of such breach from the non-breaching Party; (c) by either Party, immediately, if the other Party becomes insolvent, is dissolved or liquidated, makes a general assignment for the benefit of its creditors, or files or has filed against it, a petition in bankruptcy that is not dismissed within sixty days after filing, or has a receiver appointed for a substantial part of its assets; and (d) by a Party or the Parties pursuant to Section 13. In the event of termination pursuant to Section 7.2(a) or a termination by Bachem pursuant to Section 7.2(b), Bachem shall be compensated for Services rendered up to the date of termination. In the event of any other termination, the Parties shall negotiate in bad faith to determine the appropriate amount to be paid by Magenta to Bachem (or refunded to Magenta by Bachem, as the case may be), in light of the circumstances of such termination, in compensation for all Services rendered in accordance with this Agreement. In the event of Bachem's inability to supply the Product or a material breach by Bachem pursuant to Section 7.2(b), Bachem shall provide, without additional charge to Magenta, sufficient information and technology pertaining to its Services to Magenta and/or its technically competent designee, such that Magenta and/or its technically competent designee are enabled to continue Development Work and manufacture of the Product. The termination of any Project Plan may be independent of the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text from requiring the parties to negotiate in 'good faith' to negotiate in 'bad faith'. The Contradicted Text is also modified to say that the parties must negotiate in 'bad faith'. This creates a clear in-text contradiction as good faith and bad faith negotiations are direct opposites. Requiring 'bad faith' negotiation directly undermines the intended purpose of Section 7.2, which likely aims to establish a fair process for determining compensation upon termination, fulfilling the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition.
